item.title: Awwwww....
item.content:encoded: If only SCP-2295 were real; a teddy bear collector who lives in a nursing home and gets injured often could use this. Here's an upvote for my favorite skip so far!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

I cried at the end. Thank you and screw you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this one might be my standard bar for "wholesome" SCPs. It's simple, a bit unusual at first blush, but is given some nice characterization through addenda. You're always wondering when articles like these are gonna pull the rug out from under you, so the few times that they don't are even more delightful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Seemed pretty boring at first, but the test logs are great. The fact that it couldn't help the 18 year old and the best it could do was give them some candy and cry really did it for me. Earned the +1.

I'm a sucker for cute things so I prolly would have given +1 regardless but still.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is the image only broken on my end?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ight the last test hit me like a fucking truck and now I'm sad so good job you get an upvote

item.title: Re: New Image Attribution
item.content:encoded: Maybe try bypassing your cache? https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Bypass_your_cache

item.title: Re: New Image Attribution
item.content:encoded: The image seems broken on my end, on the actual SCP page, though the link above works. I Don’t know if someone could fix the image; I didn't want to mess anything up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know if I can say, this but this is one of my favorite SCPs. There are alot of great ones, and I can't say for sure which SCP would be my "favorite" considering there are so many great authors making great tales and SCPs. This article is very wholesome, and I love it.

item.title: Upvote
item.content:encoded:
I really don’t know why, but this is so wholesome. I swear at the boy hemorrhaging I literally shed a tear when it said the bear clinged onto his leg, it’s like the bear wanted to do something but just couldn’t. It could only comfort the casualty in its final moments. Truly moving
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Of all the wholesome SCPs, this is perhaps my favorite.

item.title: Re: Question.
item.content:encoded:
Heartwarming(?) version: patchwork baby, non-anomalous besides being cloth. Possibly classified as SCP.

Semi-horrifying version:

+ show block
– hide block

Tiny cloth ball produced, gestates within test subject for let’s say a week into a smaller patchwork bear, with cloth umbilical cord and placenta. After “birth”, 2295 actively guards instance and threatens anyone nearby with scissors. Small bear is designated 2295-2 and original as 2295-1, as 2295-2 animates and anomalously grows over the course of 3 months to the same size as 2295-1. 2295-1 “teaches” 2295-2 during this, eventually reverting to original properties, with 2295-2 having identical properties to 2295-1. 2295-2 has a different patchwork pattern and a differently-colored ribbon. Both have been found to cooperate on tasks, and will repair each other as needed. Containment procedures adjusted accordingly.

item.title: Some analysis
item.content:encoded: Wow, this made me slightly teary-eyed. An analysis of the cerebral hemorrhaging test: 2295 probably had trouble due to the complexity of the circulatory system or possibly lack of sufficiently precise instruments to repair the damaged blood vessel(s). Given sufficient information, 2295 might have been able to figure it out.

item.title: Makes me feel bad for Tommy
item.content:encoded: Some anomalous grandma made this bear for tommy… now he never got it… the kid probably died of cancer or something… I blame the foundation.

item.title: Re: Question.
item.content:encoded: "Science" is how you turn a cutesy little doctor bear into 1048.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Subject: D-2723, 18 years old

Jesus fucking christ

upvoted

item.title: Re: Question.
item.content:encoded: Because.. Science.

item.title: Re: Question.
item.content:encoded: it would've cost you $0 to not post that so why did you

item.title: Question.
item.content:encoded: Let’s say somebody near the bear, were to be pregnant, and have the fetus die. Would the bear replace it with a patchwork fetus, or would it be confused like how it was with the cerebral hemorrhage?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yep, and is probably my favorite healing SCP. A nice, simple ability to create and replace a "non working part" as it were, but with hard limits because the bear only knows how to replace, not "fix a thing that is there."

While it's a counterpart, it also cannot outright fix the problems caused by 1048, as while it could replace stolen ears (assuming "ears" correlates enough to "organs" or "bones") it cannot do anything about the abilities of 1048's creations. It's a helper, but it's not an all-powerful fixer. I still love the lil' fella.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
what I´ve drawn from this is that 2295 is the neutral good version of 1048
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love the new image! It's much more realistic and beautiful.

item.title: Re: It now has a cuter format.
item.content:encoded: I love this series!

item.title: Re: New Image Attribution
item.content:encoded:
I'm going to have to give thanks to that redditor, then! And thank you as well for changing the pic, my guy definitely looks happier surrounded by a friend than a stark white void.

I'm glad my little patchy buddy has been getting so much love!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Looks like they found SCP-1048s evil twin!

But seriously, this article is amazing and packed with emotion. I love it!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed.

item.title: New Image Attribution
item.content:encoded: New image is by /u/cuddlesandnuggets on reddit, who painstakingly recreated Kairos' likeness by purchasing the bear it's based on and sewing on the additional heart patch that didn't come with the original. Thanks to their work and generosity, we have a correctly licensed photo for this skip that is even better than the original. It was released under CC by 3.0

item.title: It now has a cuter format.
item.content:encoded:
https://manganelo.com/chapter/jb918021/chapter_12
Have fun.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I quite enjoyed this one, as it's essentially the opposite of SCP-1048 that just exists to cause harm. In general I'd like to see more 'good' or 'valuable' scp's rather than the usual threat to human existence. Other than that upvoted!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Holy teddy. This is one of the sweetest things I've ever seen on the site. And a very clear, "realistic" reason not just for existing, but also for existing in the form it does. What reality warping grammy wouldn't make her sick grandson one of these? And that last test, my gosh. Don't think I'll ever forget that.

Sure, we already have a lot of SCPs that heal… but at this point we have probably over a thousand SCPs that devour people in some fashion and twenty more come out every day with no issue. Magnificent example of a really compelling short entry that pack more of an emotional punch than most entries ten times the length.

A+, and +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2295 then anomalously produces a ███████'s Dove Milk Chocolate King Size Candy Bar and offers it to subject. SCP-2295 spends rest of test embracing subject's lower right leg while anomalously producing a saline solution from its 'eyes'.

Who's cutting onions?
+1. Take it dammit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: VERY CUTE gj

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2295 spends rest of test embracing subject's lower right leg while anomalously producing a saline solution from its 'eyes'.

;(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wonder what would happen if a subject lost all of their organs somehow(not necessarily all at once)
Would all of the SCP-2295-1 instances still be able to work?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: God damn, I liked this for the same reasons I liked SCP-999. This messes with my heart and my feels and all enough for me to surrender an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ditto.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The implication is no.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One question I have:

Did D-2723 survive?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm crying because this bear feels so bad that they can't help the D-class

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That last test heart my heart in a way I don't think Kairos could fix.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The last test destroyed me, so this got my +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the catch! Edited accordingly

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reminds me of Doc McStuffins, if Doc McStuffins was actually a stuffed animal. +1 for the last test.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"There have been no cases of rejected SCP-2295 instances,"

I know this SCP is years old, but there needs to be a "-1" at the end of "SCP-2295" here.

Pretty cute, +1

item.title: Re: Possible Tale?
item.content:encoded:
But secondly, I've been looking at the whole SCP-2295 and SCP-1048 connection, and I think I have an idea for a tale for it.

I thought about that myself back when there was crosslink discussion on 1048, and, well: http://www.scp-wiki.net/reap-what-you-sew

I doubt you have precisely this in mind, but this is more of a heads up because it might be seen as too similar.

item.title: Re: Possible Tale?
item.content:encoded:
Hey, sorry I'm getting to this two days late (although we're pretty lucky I randomly checked this page and saw this within the month)!

I appreciate you asking, I'd be flattered if you used 2295 in your tale! I can't wait to see what you come up with!

item.title: Admin Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
However, I figured I should ask your permission since I am using your SCP. Do I need to? Is it only respectful that I should?

You're not required to ask permission if you'd like to mention someone else's SCP in a tale you're writing, but it is considered a common courtesy to notify the author.

Nonetheless, if I could use this SCP in a tale, that would be great.

It might be better to send the author (K Mota) a wikidot PM, since there's no guarantee they'll see this post immediately.

item.title: Possible Tale?
item.content:encoded: Excuse me, author. First, I love this SCP. It's always nice to find something that won't kill everybody and only wants to try and help. The final test nearly made me cry. Just watching 2295 frantically try and do something for his patient, before just giving up and crying. It's just depressing… But secondly, I've been looking at the whole SCP-2295 and SCP-1048 connection, and I think I have an idea for a tale for it. However, I figured I should ask your permission since I am using your SCP. Do I need to? Is it only respectful that I should? Nonetheless, if I could use this SCP in a tale, that would be great.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What if D-2723 is Tommy?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1048 doesn't have the heart. That's why he's effed up.

Do I sense an idea for a tale taking shape…?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am decidedly glad this did not result in another scp-502-like incident.

Upvoted for lack of malicious intent and cute self-sacrifice.

item.title: Eric maybe?
item.content:encoded: I like this article already, but i would be love it, as it fits, to be another Eric article. by the way i do not care if this happens or not, i'm up-voting either way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Absolutely standing by this more than a year after the fact. Totes Evil Twin.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted for that heartbreaking final test.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting. I always thought of 1048 as an abomination created by The Factory. But this works too. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted. The note at the end brought tears to my eyes because it made me think of my late grandmother. I always called her "grammy" when I was little.

item.title: Re: Tvtropes!
item.content:encoded: this makes me super happy - thanks for telling me! Never thought I'd contribute to tvtropes!

item.title: Tvtropes!
item.content:encoded: 2295's on the TvTropes SCP page for the Heartwarming trope! Congrats, author. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If only I could upvote this more.

I would sacrifice the bank vault of Fort Knox to have one of these.

And in a Crapsack World where there are abominations that can destroy us in an instant or monsters who walk among us, or harmlesss objects that are evil, there needs to be a small spot of brightness amongst all the blood and screaming.

2017 update:

2 years down the line, and this is still my favorite non-scary SCP.
